elements.sent-2411-001: Magnetohydrodynamics (MHD) waves have been employed to explain the simultaneous upper and lower kilohertz (kHz) QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-002: Nevertheless, the intricate and undefined formation pathways of twin kHz QPOs present a compelling avenue for exploration.
elements.sent-2411-003: This area of study holds great interest as it provides an opportunity to derive crucial parameters related to compact stars.
elements.sent-2411-004: The seed photons can be transported through a high temperature corona and Compton up-scattered.
elements.sent-2411-005: The variability of the photons with the frequencies of twin MHD waves can lead to the observed twin kHz QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-006: Quasi-Periodic Oscillations (QPOs) have been identified in many kinds of celestial objects including the Sun, stars, magnetars, neutron star low-mass X-ray binaries (NS-LMXBs), black hole LMXBs and active galactic nuclei \citep{van2006,Nakariakov2009,Shi2018 .}
elements.sent-2411-007: However, the fastest X-ray variability, known as kilohertz QPOs (kHz QPOs), in NS-LMXBs have been paid more attention because the high frequencies of QPOs might be related to some effects of general relativity.
elements.sent-2411-008: KHz QPOs often emerge in pairs in the power spectra of NS-LMXBs, which are named as twin kHz QPOs including the upper and lower kHz QPOs according to their frequencies ($f_{\rm u}$ and $f_{\rm l}$).
elements.sent-2411-009: Initially, the difference in the frequencies between the twin kHz QPOs appeared relatively constant, leading to the proposal of the beat-frequency model \citep{Miller1998 .}
elements.sent-2411-010: In that model and subsequent models, it needs to be explained why the difference of twin kHz QPOs' frequencies is not a stochastic value and why they can emerge in the same time.
elements.sent-2411-011: Furthermore, \citet{Barret2005 and \citet{Ribeiro2017 posited that the origin of the upper QPOs was likely different from the lower QPOs due to the difference in some properties, including the fraction root-mean-square amplitude (hereafter rms), quality factors, time-lag between soft and hard X-ray photons.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: Therefore, a comprehensive explanation should account for both the relationship between these phenomena and their distinguishing characteristics.
elements.sent-2411-013: In LMXBs, the available models of QPOs can be mainly divided into two categories: dynamic mechanism and radiation origin.
elements.sent-2411-014: Generally, QPOs are supposed to be produced in an accretion disc or the surface of a corona, and numerous dynamic models have been proposed to account for their occurrence \citep[e.g.][]{Miller1998,Stella1999,Osherovich1999,Abramowicz2003,Shi2009,Shi2010,Shi20102,Ingram2011,Shi2014,Shi2018 .}
elements.sent-2411-015: In the context of radiation-based mechanisms, several key questions revolve around the specific locations where QPOs are generated and how they are ultimately produced in X-ray emissions.
elements.sent-2411-016: This involves understanding the connection between the aforementioned dynamic models and the actual generation of X-rays.
elements.sent-2411-017: Thus a corona is often considered as the place where both the hard X-ray photons and QPOs originate.
elements.sent-2411-018: It indicates that QPOs may depend on the states of the system and the rms of QPOs changes in different states.
elements.sent-2411-019: The increasing rms of QPOs with the increasing photon energy and a possible saturation or decline were observed in several works \citep{Berger1996, Yu1997, Gilfanov2003, Mukherjee2012 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: The relation between the rms of QPOs and Comptonizing process were also discussed \citep[e.g. ][]{Lee1998, Zdziarski2005 .}
elements.sent-2411-021: Then the Kompaneets equation was calculated in order to understand the energy dependence of rms and time lag of the lower kHz QPOs \citep{Kumar2014, Karpouzas2020 .}
elements.sent-2411-022: However, a fundamental question that remains unanswered is how the simultaneous twin kHz QPOs are coupled into the X-ray radiation.
elements.sent-2411-023: The variability in the Sun similar to QPOs are often called quasi-periodic pulsations (QPPs) and are often seen in different wave bands from radio to hard X-ray.
elements.sent-2411-024: QPPs have been attributed to potential origins in magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) oscillations, which may arise from standing modes within plasma structures or wave dispersion \citep{Nakariakov2009 .}
elements.sent-2411-025: The mechanism of QPOs in a NS-LMXB may be similar to that in the Sun, that is to say, QPOs may also be related to magnetohydrodynamics.
elements.sent-2411-026: However, it's essential to note that significant disparities exist in the gravitational forces and magnetic fields between the Sun and compact stars, thus the different values of gravity and magnetic field may lead to difference in the quasi-periods for QPPs in the Sun and QPOs in XBs.
elements.sent-2411-027: A series of our previous works have been done on the relation between the frequencies of kHz QPOs and the accretion disc in LMXBs.
elements.sent-2411-028: Then the relation between an accretion rate and the frequencies of kHz QPOs were obtained by twin MHD waves \citet{Shi2014, Shi2018 .}
elements.sent-2411-029: In order to explain the rms of twin kHz QPOs and combine the dynamic mechanism with the radiation process, we need to explore the physical process how twin MHD waves are produced at the innermost radius of an accretion disc and transported into the corona around a NS along with magnetic field lines \citep{Shi2009, Shi2014, Shi2018 .}
elements.sent-2411-030: In order to answer the above fundamental question, we combine the connection and the difference between upper and lower kHz QPOs, that is to say, we suppose that QPOs can be produced in the same dynamic mechanism but the twin QPO signals originate from different regions of the corona around the NS in the XB.
elements.sent-2411-031: The MHD waves interact with the plasma in the corona and lead to some oscillations (e.g. the heating rates, the temperature of electrons, the number density of photons) based on quasi-steady X-ray emission described by Kompaneets equation.
elements.sent-2411-032: Finally, the oscillations of the number density of emission photons lead to the X-ray variabilities in a power spectrum.
elements.sent-2411-033: In this work, the basic physics to generate twin kHz QPOs in a corona and the basic equations to describe twin kHz QPOs including Kompaneets equation are introduced in Sect. 2.
elements.sent-2411-034: In Sect. 3, the observational data from \citet{Zhang2017 are revisited in order to compare with our calculation by Monte Carlo method.}
elements.sent-2411-035: The rms of 28 groups of twin kHz QPOs in 4U 1636$-$53 are also obtained.
elements.sent-2411-036: Our discussion and conclusions about twin kHz QPOs are in the last section.
elements.section-2411-002: Twin kHz QPOs produced in a corona
elements.sent-2411-037: Studies by \citet{Shi2009, Shi2010 and \citet{Shi2014, Shi2018 have unveiled the presence of two distinct modes in magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) waves, offering an explanation for the concurrent upper and lower kHz QPOs.}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Similar to the twin kHz QPOs, the twin MHD waves are named as the upper and the lower MHD wave according to their frequencies.
elements.sent-2411-039: Originating within the innermost regions of the accretion disc in a LMXB, these MHD waves propagate into the corona enveloping the NS within the LMXB (see Fig. 1).
elements.sent-2411-040: As shown in \citet{Somov2012 , the upper MHD waves with higher frequencies can be transported in a shorter distance than the lower MHD waves (e.g. for slightly damped MHD waves, Landau damping).}
elements.sent-2411-041: To comprehend the intricate process underlying the generation of variability, we develop a radiation model wherein the longer wavelength mode permeates the NS surface entirely, while the shorter wavelength mode solely perturbs the outer layers, that is to say, the upper kHz QPOs emanate from the outermost layer of the corona, whereas the lower QPOs arise from the entire corona.
elements.sent-2411-042: Finally, the radiation from Compton up-scattering will be changed in the form of a quasi-periodic variability due to oscillations from the MHD waves.
elements.sent-2411-043: Consequently, the QPOs are posited to originate from the oscillating physical parameters of the plasma throughout the entire corona or from a specific segment of the corona influenced by disturbances from various physical processes associated with X-ray radiation in a quasi-steady state, which is the same to the steady radiation in the work of \citet{Kumar2014 and \citet{Karpouzas2020 .}}
elements.sent-2411-044: A quasi-steady state is introduced here because maintaining an absolute steady state is challenging.
elements.sent-2411-045: The mass accretion rate's variability can affect the properties of the corona's surface region.
elements.sent-2411-046: However, the quasi-steady state of the radiation field expands to most of the corona and it is similar to the quasi-statics in the thermodynamics.
elements.sent-2411-047: This quasi-steady state can be maintained because the accumulation of accreting matter takes time and the macro parameters of the accretion system will hardly be changed in a time longer than milliseconds (the time scale of kHz QPOs).
elements.sent-2411-048: To align with the terminology used by \citet{Kumar2014 and \citet{Karpouzas2020 , we will refer to this quasi-steady state simply as the steady state below.}}
elements.sent-2411-049: In principle, these perturbations can be generated by other mechanism, but the twin MHD waves are an attractive candidate as we have already shown that they generate roughly the correct frequencies \citep{Shi2009, Shi2014, Shi2018 .}
elements.sent-2411-050: In addition, the twin MHD waves offer an explanatory framework for why the two modes of twin kHz QPOs can be produced at different parts of the corona.

elements.sent-2411-052: Then we explore this model by perturbed Kompaneets equation for two layers of a corona below.

elements.subsection-2411-002: Equations on the steady radiation and on the variabilities produced by MHD waves
elements.sent-2411-062: The twin kHz QPOs are produced by the oscillations from the disturbances of the MHD waves superposed on a steady X-ray radiation.
elements.sent-2411-063: After the MHD waves are transported into the corona, they will change the heating rates because the MHD waves can heat the plasma in the corona and thus produce oscillations.
elements.sent-2411-064: The change of the heating rate will change the electron temperature and other parameters.
elements.sent-2411-065: Then those disturbed parameters will change the final X-ray radiation and twin kHz QPOs will appear.
elements.sent-2411-066: Now the result after the disturbances will be introduced in the steady state below.

elements.subsection-2411-007: The physics behind the relations between the parameters
elements.sent-2411-189: The intricate relationship between kHz QPOs and the accretion-radiation processes within NS-LMXBs has been a subject of extensive exploration.
elements.sent-2411-190: KHz QPOs, characterized by their high-frequency nature, are considered as valuable probes into the underlying physics governing accretion onto NSs.
elements.sent-2411-191: Their frequency is intricately connected to the accretion rate, a crucial parameter dictating the innermost radius of the accretion disc, and the innermost radius of the accretion disc plays a pivotal role in determining the frequency of kHz QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-192: The higher the accretion rate, the smaller the innermost radius, leading to an increase in the frequency of these oscillations.
elements.sent-2411-193: Several works, including those by \citet{Mendez1999,Mendez2001 , \citet{Straaten2002 , \citet{Done2007 and \citet{Altamirano2008 , have investigated the intricate connection between the innermost radius of the accretion disc and the X-ray luminosity of the source.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-194: Further exploration by \citet{Zhang2017 , \citet{Sanna2013 and \citet{Lyu2014 in the context of 4U 1636$-$53 has enriched our understanding of the interplay between spectral and timing properties.}}}
elements.sent-2411-195: Based on the simulation results presented in Sect. 3, we have illustrated the accretion and radiation processes for kHz QPOs in NS-LMXBs, depicting both low and high accretion rate scenarios in Fig. 6.
elements.sent-2411-196: The effect of changes in the corona's size relative to other parameters is not discussed here because no clear correlation has been found.
elements.sent-2411-197: Additionally, the transition between states, driven by the accumulation of material through the accretion process, requires a considerable amount of time.
elements.sent-2411-198: In the low accretion state, the accretion disc is truncated by a strong magnetic field with a larger magnetosphere, and the NS exhibits a lower surface temperature covered by a higher temperature corona.
elements.sent-2411-199: In contrast, under high accretion rate, the magnetosphere experiences more intense compression, leading to a reduction in the magnetospheric radius.
elements.sent-2411-200: Consequently, the accretion disc moves closer to the NS, and the NS, now with a higher surface temperature, is covered by a lower temperature corona.
elements.sent-2411-201: In panel (a) of Fig. 2, the flux of the source increases with the rising temperature of seed photons ($kT_{\rm b}$).
elements.sent-2411-202: This correlation can be well described by an exponential function.
elements.sent-2411-203: This suggests that, as the accretion rate increases, the higher temperature of seed photons in the corona around the NS enhances the radiation of X-rays through Compton up-scattering.
elements.sent-2411-204: As illustrated in Eq. (1), the injection rate of seed photons per unit volume increases with the ascending $kT_{\rm b}$.
elements.sent-2411-205: The approximate relation (${\dot{n _{s\gamma}\sim E^2*e^{-\frac{E {kT_{b }$) can be derived.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-206: Consequently, more seed photons are scattered in the corona, and their energy is boosted by the scattering process.
elements.sent-2411-207: This leads to an exponential rise in flux from the scattering with increasing $kT_{\rm b}$.
elements.sent-2411-208: In contrast, the temperature of electrons in the corona decreases with the rising $kT_{\rm b}$, as depicted in panel (b) of Fig. 2, a phenomenon also discussed by Zhang et al. (2017).
elements.sent-2411-209: As explained earlier, the injection rate of seed photons increases with growing $kT_{\rm b}$, leading to a higher number density of photons in the corona.
elements.sent-2411-210: The increased photon density intensifies the cooling process of the corona due to the positive correlation between the cooling rate ($\int_{E_{\rm min ^{E_{\rm max }\frac{n_{\gamma{ 0 {\sigma{ _{\rm T {m_{\rm e c (4kT_{\rm e -E)EdE$) and the photon density.}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-211: In other words, the temperature ($kT_{\rm e}$) decreases when the rising Compton cooling rate per electron surpasses the heating rate, as per Eq. (4).
elements.sent-2411-212: Up to now, it is an open question how much depth MHD waves can be transported in the plasma accompanied with the strong magnetic field of a NS, and solving the problem will build up more knowledge of the relationships between the parameters.
elements.sent-2411-213: This endeavor will advance the understanding of the interaction between plasma and MHD waves, such as the propagation length of MHD waves, the frequencies of kHz QPOs and the heating rate of the plasma from MHD waves.

elements.sent-2411-235: Although the flare-related QPPs take place in the Sun but the QPOs take place in NS-LMXBs, they may have the same physical process but are shown in different wave bands, which should originate from different radiation mechanisms (e.g. gyrosynchrotron in the Sun and Compton up-scattering in NS-LMXBs).
elements.sent-2411-236: First, the arched magnetic field lines in the corona of the Sun are similar to the dipolar magnetic field lines of NSs.
elements.sent-2411-237: The magnetic field with arched field lines can make particles to be speeded up or down, which leads to that electromagnetic waves (e.g. X-rays) are produced.
elements.sent-2411-238: Then MHD waves lead to the oscillation of the magnetic field in a large region in a corona and some similar oscillation may also be produced in NS-LMXBs and the Sun.
elements.sent-2411-239: Finally, the dynamic interaction of waves and energetic particles appears in the region and variabilities of X-ray can be produced.
elements.sent-2411-240: According to the analysis, it can be guessed that the magnetic field with an arched field lines may be needed to produce QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-241: However, the oscillations from different origins may lead to different types of QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-242: In the Sun, the short-period QPPs detected in radio/microwave emissions are considered to be related to the dynamic interaction of waves and energetic particles, while the long-period QPPs observed in white light, UV and EUV wavebands are usually thought to be associated with the dynamics of plasma.
elements.sent-2411-243: In brief, waves interacting with the plasmas and produced by dynamics may be an origin of QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-244: The reported arched quasi-periodic fast magnetoacoustic waves propagating away from the flare site (Liu et al. 2011) were found to be consistent with the periodicity of QPPs in the flare light curve, which may suggest an origin of QPPs.
elements.sent-2411-245: It help us to understand QPOs in LMXBs and other celestial bodies.
elements.sent-2411-246: However, the gravity magnetoacoustic waves as MHD waves may be a promising origin of QPOs due to the very strong gravity and magnetic field of a NS.
elements.sent-2411-247: Here the frequencies of QPPs and QPOs ($\nu$) can be compared and estimated by using the expression of the frequency of Alf\'{v en waves ($kV_{\rm A}\ \sim\ V_{\rm A /r_{\rm c}$), which has the similar frequency to magnetoacoustic waves ($\omega^2\sim\frac{1 {2 k^2(V_{\rm A ^2+C_{\rm S ^2)$), where $k$, $V_{\rm A}$, $\omega$, $C_{\rm S}$, $r_{\rm c}$ are the wave number, the Alf\'{v en speed, the angular frequency and the sonic speed of a MHD wave, the characteristic length of the magnetic field lines, respectively.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-248: The frequency ratio of kHz QPOs to QPPs ($10^2 \sim 10^6$) is consistent with the ratio of $kV_{\rm A}$ ($10^2 \sim 10^8$) that is obtained by substituting the relations ($V_{\rm A =B/\sqrt{\mu \rho}$, $\rho \sim n $) and the characteristic parameters in Table 1 into the frequency expression, where $B$, $\mu$, $\rho$, $n$ are the characteristic magnetic field, magnetic conductivity, density and number density of plasma, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-249: This ratio supports the connection between the kHz QPOs and the frequencies of QPPs.
elements.sent-2411-250: It's worth noting that this correlation does not exclude the possibility of oscillations in the plasma being generated by other physical processes, such as rotation, magnetic reconnection, or precession, within the mechanism of QPOs.

elements.sent-2411-251: According to this work, lower QPOs are produced in the whole corona but upper QPOs are produced in the outmost layer of the corona.
elements.sent-2411-252: The seed photons producing the upper QPOs are from the radiation in the inner layer and harder than the BB seed photons from the NS on average.
elements.sent-2411-253: It was also reported that scattering in the upper layers of a NS corona produced hardening of the spectra (Lapidus et al. 1986, London et al. 1986).
elements.sent-2411-254: Thus the average energy of the last photons producing the upper QPOs should be higher than that of the photons for the lower QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-255: It may be the key reason that the lower QPOs have different rms from the upper QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-256: In addition, most of the soft photons from the inner layer will travel a longer distance than hard photons and thus many soft photons will take a longer time in order to arrive at the surface of the corona, which can partially explain the time-lag between soft and hard X-ray photons.
elements.sent-2411-257: These two characteristics are the key differences between the upper QPOs and the lower QPOs, which will be studied in a next work.
elements.sent-2411-258: Finally, a single MHD wave, which may be an origin of the single kHz QPO \citep{Shi2014 , can also make the physical quantities perturbed and leads to the kHz X-ray variability.}
elements.sent-2411-259: Therefore the radiation mechanism of the single kHz QPOs can be further investigated based on the findings of this study.
elements.sent-2411-260: In this work we combine the dynamic mechanism and radiation process of the twin kHz QPOs described by Compton up-scattering, in which 28 twin kHz QPOs in 4U 1636$-$53 are considered.
elements.sent-2411-261: The parameters in the corona around the NS in the LMXB for the 28 twin kHz QPOs are obtained by Monte Carlo method.
elements.sent-2411-262: Our conclusions can be summarized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-263: The interaction between MHD waves and plasma in one part of a corona can be explored by layering the corona.
elements.sent-2411-264: QPOs can be considered as a disturbance superimposed on top of the radiation of the radiation field in a quasi-steady state.
elements.sent-2411-265: Twin disturbances from twin MHD waves produced at the innermost radius of an accretion disc can be considered as the origin of QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-266: Seed photons may be transported through a high temperature corona and scattered by Compton up-scattering mechanism.
elements.sent-2411-267: Finally, the variability of radiation photons with the frequencies of the twin MHD waves will lead to the observed twin kHz QPOs.
elements.sent-2411-268: According to this model, the calculated flux of the source increases sensitively with the increasing temperature of seed photons.
elements.sent-2411-269: In addition, rms ratio of lower QPOs to that of upper QPOs increases slightly with the increasing temperature of seed photons.
elements.sent-2411-270: Considering the chosen best parameters, the weak relations between $k T_{\rm e}$ and $f$, $f_{\rm L /f_{\rm U}$, ${\rm RMSL /{\rm RMSU}$ are obtained.}}
elements.sent-2411-271: With the increasing temperature of electrons, all the three parameters decreases, which shows that the observational characteristics of QPOs are possibly closely linked to the accretion state of the source.
elements.sent-2411-272: In addition, the negative correlations are also shown between $k T_{\rm e}$ and $k T_{\rm b}$.
elements.sent-2411-273: In summary, twin kHz QPOs can be considered to originate from the oscillating physical parameters of the plasma in a whole corona and in a part of the corona.
elements.sent-2411-274: Twin kHz QPOs may be a key tool to decide the innermost radius of an accretion disc, study the characteristics of state in LMXBs, test effect of general relativity, estimate parameters of compact stars and explore the new MHD waves (gravity magnetoacoustic waves).
